# Distribution / Host Integration SSOT

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

Executable SSOT: [`distribution-host-integration.riido.json`](distribution-host-integration.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `distribution-host-docs`.

C11 Distribution / Host Integration owns distribution channel identity, Store/App helper boundaries, local IPC facts, Store review/demo mode, and host-facing privacy metadata.

Reader documentation is generated from this manifest while detailed policy tables and metadata allowlists remain generated by their dedicated evidence tools.

## Parts

- [Invariants](distribution-host-integration/invariants.md)
- [Local IPC](distribution-host-integration/local-ipc.md)
- [Store Channel Policy](distribution-host-integration/store-channel-policy.md)

- [Store Distribution Architecture](../30-architecture/store-distribution.md)
- [Open Questions](../50-roadmap/open-questions.md)

## Executable Assertions

- C11 owns host distribution facts, but does not own provider process execution or SaaS assignment state.
- Store channel policy decisions are generated from executable policy code and store contract inputs.
- Review/demo mode is offline, local-only, and cannot enable provider execution or telemetry sync.
- Q-DIST-001 resolved / Q-DIST-001 RESOLVED: Mac App Store external provider CLI execution requires user-selected or sandbox/security-scoped OS grant plus Store review approval.
- Q-CTX-005 Store App repository / adapter ownership is closed in C11/store architecture docs; future desktop/app repository remains outside this public daemon repository.
- Store App GUI must remain a client of C11/local API contracts and provider process 를 직접 spawn 하지 않는다.
- Store App distribution surfaces provider CLI 를 bundle / download / silently install 하지 않는다.
- Only unresolved store-channel questions remain in the roadmap; resolved decisions move into generated domain docs.
